name: set_component_reference
description: Set a serialized field (reference) on a component attached to a GameObject. This works like dragging and dropping in the Unity Inspector. Can assign scene GameObjects, Components, or project assets (prefabs, materials, sprites, ScriptableObjects, etc.). Use 'source_game_object' for scene objects or 'asset_path' for project assets. The tool automatically resolves the correct type to match the field.

target_game_object.description: The name of the GameObject that has the component with the field to set.

target_component.description: The type name of the component that contains the field, e.g. 'ScoreManager', 'PlayerController'.

field_name.description: The name of the serialized field to set, e.g. 'pointsCounter', 'playerRigidbody', 'resourcePrefab'. Use the C# field name, not the Inspector display name.

source_game_object.description: The name of a scene GameObject to assign (or to find the component on). Mutually exclusive with 'asset_path'.

asset_path.description: Path to a project asset to assign, e.g. 'Assets/Prefabs/Resource.prefab', 'Assets/Materials/Wood.mat', 'Assets/Sprites/Icon.png'. Use this for prefabs, materials, sprites, ScriptableObjects, and other project assets. Mutually exclusive with 'source_game_object'.

source_component.description: Optional. The specific component type name to get from the source, e.g. 'TextMeshProUGUI', 'Rigidbody'. If omitted, the tool will auto-detect the correct type based on the field's type.
